growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.agit: 225 rpm (Halobacterium strain 004.1 rotary shaker); other halophile growth-rate cultures shaken/rotary
growth_records.medium: Modified Payne's medium supplemented with NaCl (Payne et al. 1960)
growth_records.cite: Stevenson et al. Is there a common water-activity limit for the three domains of life?. The ISME Journal (2015). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.219. [PMC4438321]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Doubling times for halophilic Archaea and Bacteria in media supplemented with NaCl...Halobacterium noricense (DSM 15987): ~14 Days at water activity 0.717 (and 0.687)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 reports approximate doubling time '~14 Days' at water activity 0.717 in modified Payne's medium + NaCl, 37C, 2 replicates, monitored turbidometrically over 2 months. mu calculated as ln2/(14 days x24 h/day)=0.00206 h-1. Value is an approximation (~) from the source; exact NaCl concentration for aw=0.717 not given in main text (only referenced Supplementary Table S4, not available).
